query: What books by Moojan Momen do you have?
response: The library holds 146 works by Moojan Momen, showcasing his extensive contributions to Bahá'í scholarship. Some notable titles include *Mazhar-i Ilahi (Manifestation of God)* and *Memorials of the Faithful: The Democratization of Sainthood*. His work, [*An Introduction to Shi'i Islam: The History and Doctrines of Twelver Shi'ism*](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Baha'i%20Books/moojan-momen_an-introduction-to-shii-islam-the-history-and-doctrines-of-twelver-shiism#p1260), delves into the complexities of Shi'i beliefs, exploring how followers ["seek salvation from God"](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Baha'i%20Books/moojan-momen_an-introduction-to-shii-islam-the-history-and-doctrines-of-twelver-shiism#p1267).

query: What Hindu scriptures do you carry?
response: The library holds 127 Hindu texts, featuring collections such as Bhakti and Devotional, and Epics. Among these, you can find works like [Gitanjali (Song Offerings)](https://oceanlibrary.com/hymns-of-atharva-veda) by Rabindranath Tagore and [Hindu Literature - Ramayana, Nala, Sakuntala](https://oceanlibrary.com/hymns-of-atharva-veda) by various authors. The *Bhagavad Gita* offers a profound vision, where Krishna grants Arjuna ["sense divine"](https://oceanlibrary.com/hymns-of-atharva-veda) to perceive divine glory. Similarly, the *Upanishads* encourage us to free our hearts from prejudice to draw ["inspiration, love and wisdom"](https://oceanlibrary.com/hymns-of-atharva-veda).

query: How many documents are in the library total?
response: The library houses a total of 44,937 documents, spanning various religious traditions. Among these, there are 41,590 Bahá'í documents, 1,418 from Judaism, 858 Buddhist texts, 404 Islamic writings, and 208 Christian documents. The Hindu collection includes 127 texts, while Zoroastrian, Tao, Confucian, Sikh, and Jain traditions are also represented. Reflecting on the breadth of this collection, the *Dhammapada* reminds us that ["all that we are is the result of what we have thought"](https://oceanlibrary.com/dhammapada_buddha).
